🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-20 02:03 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?No — 5 items need immediate attention (1 escalate-now, 4 needs-management-attention)
Urgent vs smaller5 urgent, 6 need a nudge, 8 routine
Total open (active, excl. triage)19 +2 vs last digest (17 → 19)
1
🚨 Escalate now
4
🔴 Needs attention
6
🟡 Watch
8
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14752 — P3 Kafka forwarder lag, benko-igp-azur — acked within a minute, quiet overnight, fine so far
  • INC-14728 — P1 Pokerklas (Intercom) — deposits reporting 0 since 17 Jul; root cause (partner stopped sending deposit events) found and communicated to partner within ~1h45m, well handled

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • none
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 1

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 54d· evidence to 09 Jul 09:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

All three breaches behind the 🚨 flag (A2/A4, B5, C10/D15) still hold with zero change this cycle. This reflects the 16 Jul management note (H2Bet accepted the workaround) but the note does not resolve the underlying breaches. Now 54.4 days open.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed as root cause within hours of the 26 May report, but 54 days later there is still no deployed fix — roughly 300 duplicate grants/week continue. Per the management note, H2Bet has accepted the interim workaround and REW-176 remains parked on Rewards-team capacity until mid-August. Zero new activity anywhere again this cycle.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: still holds — 54+ days in, no decisive fix deployed, REW-176 still parked
  • B5: still holds — zero evidence of any BetConstruct response or outreach progress since 18 Jun
  • C10/D15: still holds — Intercom conversation remains closed while incident.io stays open P1 with no incident.io-side explanation of the closure or REW-176 status
Proposed / pending
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note), still blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug — no movement observed
Timeline (2)
  • No new Slack messages, Intercom parts, or incident.io update text since the last read — fully static again
  • REW-176 still shows no movement this cycle

→ Force a decision point: get Rewards-team capacity committed to REW-176 with a hard date, or explicitly accept the ~300/week duplicate-grant cost until mid-August and say so in the record.

🔴 Needs attention 4

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open 17d· evidence to 17 Jul 13:55 UTC
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: Solved as an incident. Only pending item is with Estelarbet, who need to identify which players were impacted — this is being checked and shared from our side; we need their input on which players complained so we can cross-check. Confirmed via the partner Intercom ticket.

Per Andrea's management note this is otherwise solved as an incident, but the partner's scope-clarifying message from 17 Jul 22:35 UTC has now sat unanswered for ~51.5h (2.1 days) with no re-engagement from FT — a clear, overdue partner-comms breach (C9/C10) that the note does not cover, still 🔴 this cycle and getting worse.

Cloudflare-origin check blocked shortlink/unsubscribe traffic; mitigated same day, fix merged 02 Jul. Per Andrea's management note this is solved as an incident, pending only Estelarbet's cross-check. The partner (Gloria Morocho) clarified on Intercom at 22:35 UTC 17 Jul that the reported error affected ALL urls (not just unsubscribe), across both notifications and emails — reframing the cross-check's scope. As of this run, ~27 hours later, FT still has not replied; the Intercom conversation remains in waiting-on-us state.

Open breaches
  • C9/C10: partner's 22:35 UTC 17 Jul scope clarification (that the reported error affected ALL urls, not just unsubscribe) remains unanswered ~51.5h later — well past what should have been a same-day reply
Proposed / pending
  • CF-origin carve-out - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - cross-check inconclusive, now needs to incorporate the partner's broader-scope clarification
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • last partner message unanswered
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly (13-15 Jul), still not fully delivered
Timeline (2)
  • 17 Jul 22:35 UTC partner (Gloria Morocho) clarifies scope: all urls affected, not only unsubscribe — no FT reply yet
  • 19 Jul run: confirmed still zero FT reply, ~27h since the clarification, no new Slack activity either

→ Reply to Estelarbet's/Gloria Morocho's 17 Jul scope-clarification message on Intercom today — over 2 days unanswered on an otherwise-solved P1.

INC-14476 slack intercomP1open 2d· evidence to 18 Jul 18:45 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Mystake Production

True P1, partner silent ~63.7h (2.7 days) despite competent internal handling (precise root cause, proactive workaround) — the breach has now persisted across three digest cycles without the previously suggested account-manager escalation being taken.

Cocobet (Mystake) blocked users appear to 'unblock' themselves because the partner's daily recon feed omits users who should stay blocked. Joachim sent the partner a clear technical ask on 17 Jul; the partner has been silent since (Intercom confirms zero new parts). Giancarlo applied an internal mitigation (origin resync + recon blacklist) that evening. Overnight, Andrea reassigned the Incident Lead a second time (Joachim → Giancarlo, 20:45 CEST 18 Jul) — but no account-manager escalation followed, despite that being the last digest's suggested action.

Open breaches
  • C9/C10: partner has not replied since being given the root-cause explanation/ask at 12:18 CEST 17 Jul — silence now ~63.7h (2.7 days); for a True P1, the account-manager escalation suggested for two prior digests has still not happened
  • D15: incident.io record still doesn't reflect the applied mitigation (origin resync + recon blacklist) or current owner
Proposed / pending
  • Resync affected origin via user migration and blacklist Cocobet's origin from the daily recon job — applied and confirmed ~19:47 CEST 17 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-17T08:42
  • root-cause explanation and recon-feed check request — delivered 12:18-12:19 CEST 17 Jul; no partner reply since, confirmed still silent through this 19 Jul run
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 10:17 UTC Joachim sends partner a technical ask — no reply since, confirmed again via Intercom this cycle (~40h silence)
  • 17 Jul 19:09-19:47 CEST Giancarlo applies internal mitigation (origin resync + recon blacklist, ~20k players)
  • 18 Jul 20:45 CEST Andrea reassigns Incident Lead Joachim → Giancarlo — no fresh partner outreach accompanied this handoff

→ Escalate to Cocobet's account manager now — this is the third consecutive digest flagging the same unaddressed suggestion on a True P1 with 63.7h of partner silence.

INC-11079 slackP2open 22d· evidence to 19 Jul 02:30 UTC
segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager (messages: 1, in: 0/s)
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: this is in reality a BetConstruct platform issue (their Operator API returns 500 on consent updates). The partner is aware and has raised it with BetConstruct themselves; active partner correspondence (examples provided, regular chasing incl. by Andrea) lives in the partner channel thread: https://fasttrack-solutions.slack.com/archives/C08VB1RBJLF/p1782549375502499 — future runs should treat that thread as the live track, not the quiet incident channel.

Management note (16 Jul) held this at 🟡 as a BetConstruct-side blocker with active chasing, with escalation if it remained unanswered much longer — that condition is now clearly met: Andrea's 16 Jul 12:54 CEST re-chase to Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) has had zero reply for ~87h (3.6 days), on a P2 open 23 days with no FT-side mitigation attempted. Corrects last cycle's duration figure (an internal miscount) but the underlying breach and 🔴 classification are unchanged. Note preserved.

Segurobet Consent Manager remains stalled since 27 Jun on partner (BetConstruct) Operator API 500s. Per management note, the live track is the partner thread, not this incident channel. No reply there since Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase to Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) — now ~9 days unanswered (partner's last message was 9 Jul). New this cycle: James Hodgson left the incident Slack channel (19 Jul 04:30 CEST).

Open breaches
  • C9/C10: BetConstruct has not responded to Andrea's 16 Jul 12:54 CEST chase in the #ftcrm-segurobet partner thread — now ~87h (3.6 days) of partner silence on an unresolved P2 open since 27 Jun (23 days); no FT-side mitigation applied in the meantime
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-27T08:36
  • last partner message unanswered
  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given; Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase remains unanswered
Timeline (3)
  • No new activity in the main incident channel or the partner thread this cycle
  • 16 Jul 12:54 CEST: Andrea's re-chase to Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) — still unanswered
  • 19 Jul 04:30 CEST: James Hodgson left the incident channel

→ Escalate directly with BetConstruct account management — 3.6 days of silence on Andrea's own re-chase, on a 23-day-old P2 with player consent updates still not applying in real time.

INC-13895 slackP2open 5d· evidence to 18 Jul 18:49 UTC
[FIRING:1] Fusion API errors Alerting (spinbet-terragon: 401 @ /external/pusher/:brand true)

P2, open 6 days, partner silent ~89.5h (3.7 days) on a direct technical ask with no re-chase attempted since 16 Jul — passive waiting per D12, still compounded by the unre-chased second lead reassignment.

Fusion API 401 errors for Spinbet Terragon are blocking on-site notifications and Rich Inbox messages, root-caused to a brand-slug mismatch. The partner has not responded to the 16 Jul 10:29 CEST slug-confirmation ask; overnight, Andrea reassigned the Incident Lead a second time (Joachim → Matteo Rapisarda), and a bot check-in nudge followed — but no re-chase to the partner has been sent, and this is now the second lead handoff without a fresh outreach attempt.

Open breaches
  • C10/D12: partner has not responded to the 16 Jul 10:29 CEST slug-confirmation ask for ~89.5h (3.7 days) and no re-chase has been sent; incident now 6 days old with player notifications still blocked for Spinbet Terragon
  • A4: second Incident Lead handoff (Joachim→Matteo) without a corresponding re-chase or new outreach action
Proposed / pending
  • Confirm definitive brand slug with partner before changing FT config - still pending, unacknowledged
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • accurate technical explanation + slug-confirmation ask sent 16 Jul 10:29 CEST
Timeline (4)
  • Status remains 'Waiting on Partner' since 14 Jul 12:34 UTC; incident now 5 days old
  • 18 Jul 20:46 CEST Andrea reassigns Incident Lead Joachim → Matteo Rapisarda
  • 18 Jul 20:49 CEST bot check-in nudge, unanswered
  • Partner slug-confirmation ask still outstanding since 16 Jul 10:29 CEST (~66h now) with no re-chase sent

→ Have Matteo Rapisarda send a fresh re-chase to Spinbet Terragon today — nearly 4 days of partner silence on a live P2 with no escalation attempted.

🟡 Watch 6

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 31d· evidence to 17 Jul 09:55 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

Continued thorough, collaborative investigation with Integration/PM engaged. The Fri-to-Mon gap was a weekend lull; it's now Monday morning and the scoping answer (Stephen/Curt) is due today — worth a check next cycle if it doesn't land, not yet a breach.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags. Robert Friberg (Integration/PM) asked Stephen/Curt to scope the update_epoch-based fallback fix on 17 Jul 10:22 CEST; no new Slack or Intercom activity since (weekend lull) — the scoping answer is still outstanding.

Proposed / pending
  • update_epoch as ordering key — Robert asked Stephen/Curt to scope size of this fallback vs pushing GiG to fix event ordering themselves; scoping answer still pending
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • structural handover to Integration/PM drafted 16 Jul, sent to Robert/Lis/Gui 17 Jul
Timeline (1)
  • No further Slack messages or Intercom parts this cycle — Stephen/Curt's scoping answer to Robert not yet posted

→ Await Stephen/Curt's scoping answer to Robert (outstanding since 17 Jul), then get a final decision on GiG escalation vs. FT-side timestamp fix.

INC-14346 slackP2open 3d· evidence to 17 Jul 21:42 UTC
CF Threat Watch: brute-force-suspect,login-success-after-fails on ft-crm.com (41.76.35.208)

Handling itself was fast and correct (B6 escalation, false-positive determination), but the record has now sat unclosed for ~3.5 days since the conclusion settled — still a minor D15 record-hygiene gap, not a live incident concern.

CF Threat Watch brute-force-suspect alert on ft-crm.com. Correct B6 security escalation flow despite paging misfires; team read is a shared-network false positive per Cloudflare's own classification. Status remains 'Investigating' with no incident.io updates since 2026-07-16T12:40:33Z; incident.io's own auto-nudge on 17 Jul asking to mark it resolved remains unactioned, reconfirmed again this cycle (zero new Slack activity).

Open breaches
  • D15: incident sitting in 'Investigating' ~3.5 days with the conclusion settled (shared-network false positive) and the automated resolve-nudge still unactioned
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 12:10 — alert fires, incident opened, escalated to P2 Investigating within minutes
  • 16 Jul 12:40 — last incident.io status update; team settles on shared-network false-positive read
  • 17 Jul 23:42 CEST — incident.io bot auto-prompts to mark resolved — still no reply as of this 19 Jul run

→ Stephen Riolo to click resolve/close now that the false-positive conclusion is settled.

INC-14331 slackP3open 3d· evidence to 18 Jul 08:36 UTC
Leonbets Activity Manager Groups Alerts Spamming

Real progress (fix proven stable) but no confirmation the durable merge has landed; the kubectl-level mitigation remains revert-exposed on the next OneClick deploy, now going on 4 days without a human update confirming merge status (A2).

Leonbets activity-manager DB-saturation alert spam; Matteo's scoped-loading fix (PR #490) proven effective over 12h+, applied via kubectl-level changes that remain exposed to reverting on the next OneClick deploy until PR #490 is merged and the env vars made durable. No substantive new activity this cycle (only a channel-join system message) — merge status for PR #490 still unconfirmed.

Open breaches
  • A2: durable fix merge (PR #490) still unconfirmed; kubectl-level mitigation remains revert-exposed on next OneClick deploy. Now ~4 days without a human update confirming merge status.
Proposed / pending

Timeline (2)

  • 18 Jul 10:37 CEST Joachim Farrugia joins the channel — no substantive update posted

→ Ask Malcolm/reviewers to confirm and prioritize merging PR #490 now rather than 'next week'.

🟢 Under control 8

8× 🟢 routine: Kafka forwarder lag (benko, acked fast), Pokerklas partner outage (well handled), Rewards GraphQL wemalaysia (self-cleared), campeon no-consumers (self-cleared), Stanleybet Romania email-scanner fix in review, CVE-2021-45046 base-image fix in progress (Patrik-reviewed, deferred to next week), Calendars door-variation fix shipped, 12play/we88 fire-late fix landed. (GiG/Power Play tag sync is 🟡, weekend lull, answer due Monday.)

INC-14728 slack intercomP1open 14h· evidence to 19 Jul 13:45 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Pokerklas Production

No breach — fast ack, fast escalation, root cause confirmed and communicated to the partner within ~1h45m, correctly identified as partner-side, follow-up task created to prevent recurrence.

Pokerklas reported deposits/conversion reporting showing 0 since 17 Jul. Joachim acked within seconds; escalated to Giancarlo within ~1h; root cause identified within ~1h45m (Pokerklas' own upstream operator stopped sending deposit payment events on 17 Jul, withdrawals unaffected). Partner informed and told to check with their dev team on Intercom at 12:59 UTC. Follow-up task created for a payment-events monitor.

Timeline (5)
  • 19 Jul 11:32 UTC P1 fires from Intercom, Joachim acks immediately
  • 19 Jul 12:40 UTC lead escalated/handed to Giancarlo Laferla
  • 19 Jul 12:59 UTC Joachim replies to partner on Intercom with confirmed root cause (missing deposit events since 17 Jul 16:51 UTC) and asks them to check internally
  • 19 Jul 13:18 UTC incident.io updated with full technical finding, status moved to Waiting on Partner
  • 19 Jul 15:45 CEST Andrea creates a follow-up task for a payment-events monitor

No breach — reporting-only false alarm confirmed, fix in review (PR #106), no player/consent impact. Quiet again this cycle.

Email security scanners triggered false unsubscribe signals for Stanleybet Romania (no emails actually suppressed). Giancarlo and the partner jointly agreed to test the fix Monday; Intercom conversation confirmed still snoozed until Mon 20 Jul 07:00 UTC, consistent with the plan. No new activity this cycle.

Proposed / pending
  • Fix in review (conversion-service-go PR #106), deploy/test Monday - agreed with partner

  • first reported 2026-07-16T12:41
  • test fix Monday — agreed with partner

campeon: no consumers on queue campeon.actions.callbackconsumer (messages: 5, in: 0/s)

No breach — alert cleared on its own, no player-facing impact evidenced, quiet resolution.

No consumers on campeon's actions callback-consumer queue (5 messages backlog). Lead James Hodgson, status Waiting on Partner. The underlying alert resolved itself overnight (19 Jul 12:14 CEST) with no further Slack activity.

  • 18 Jul 16:30 UTC alert fires, Giancarlo acks within a minute
  • 18 Jul 23:35 CEST James Hodgson traces the 403 to campeon's webhook endpoint (curl evidence posted), status → Waiting on Partner

INC-14640 slackP2open 33h· evidence to 19 Jul 18:39 UTC
[FIRING:1] Rewards: GraphQL API errors (Grafana) Alerting (true rewards wemalaysia: TIMEOUT)

No breach — alert self-resolved overnight, root cause already understood as partner-side, Monday follow-up plan stands.

Rewards GraphQL API timeout/JWT-auth errors for the wemalaysia brand. Giancarlo acked within a minute; Sam Lundborg identified the pattern as wemalaysia's own frontend occasionally sending a malformed ('Bearer null') JWT before a valid one — a known, recurring behavior on their side, not an FT bug. The underlying Grafana alert resolved itself on its own overnight (19 Jul 20:39 CEST). Team agreed to raise it with Ryan Yu Monday; lead is James Hodgson.

Proposed / pending
  • Raise JWT-generation timing with Ryan Yu Monday — agreed, not yet actioned
Timeline (4)
  • 18 Jul 16:44 UTC alert fires, Giancarlo acks within a minute
  • 18 Jul 19:08-19:13 CEST Sam Lundborg identifies root cause: wemalaysia frontend sends bad JWT before a good one — errors already stopped
  • 18 Jul 21:21 CEST status moved to Monitoring with note 'will pick this up on monday'
  • 18 Jul 22:50 CEST lead handed to James Hodgson

No breach — fix already landed, status honestly reflects 'Fixing' (D14), formal ownership in place, P3 judged leniently. Quiet again this cycle.

Patrik Potocki personally directed Agustín to ack and a Linear ticket to be opened so it can be tackled next week — an explicit, informed deprioritization. Only a routine bot resolve-nudge appeared this cycle, expected given the next-week timeline.

  • Linear ticket for next-week fix - per Patrik's direction
